title: Should the Bank of Japan Raise Interest Rates?
gist: Hamada, an intellectual architect of Abenomics, argues that conditions have fully reversed since 2013—the yen is no longer overvalued and monetary easing is no longer needed—so the Bank of Japan should hike rates, endorsing US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's recommendation.

insights.claim: At the start of 2013 when Abenomics launched, the yen was significantly overvalued and looser monetary policy was badly needed in Japan.
insights.speaker: KOICHI HAMADA
insights.so_what: Establishes the baseline against which today's conditions are judged; the framing supports a regime change in BOJ policy.
insights.quote: the yen was significantly overvalued, and looser monetary policy was badly needed

insights.claim: Conditions in Japan have now reversed from 2013, so interest-rate hikes may be the right prescription rather than continued easing.
insights.speaker: KOICHI HAMADA
insights.so_what: An Abenomics architect endorsing hikes is a meaningful signal for JGB yields and the yen; watch for BOJ normalization and a stronger yen.
insights.quote: Today, the opposite is true, indicating that interest-rate hikes may well be the right prescription for Japan.

insights.claim: Hamada endorses US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent's recommendation that Japan raise interest rates, lending an insider Japanese economist's backing to US pressure on the BOJ.
insights.speaker: KOICHI HAMADA
insights.so_what: Convergence between US Treasury and an Abenomics architect raises the probability of BOJ tightening; relevant for yen carry-trade unwind risk.
insights.locator: Should the Bank of Japan Raise Interest Rates?

insights.claim: Scott Bessent, now US Treasury Secretary under President Trump, is a long-standing student of Japanese monetary economics who repeatedly engaged with Hamada's academic work over the years.
insights.speaker: KOICHI HAMADA
insights.so_what: Signals Bessent's views on Japan are informed and deliberate, making US pressure on BOJ policy more credible and persistent.
insights.quote: His name was Scott Bessent, currently Secretary of the United States Treasury under President Donald Trump.
